reviews.author: Vladislav Barkovskij
reviews.text: We paid 50 per person. I’m not sure what exactly the fee was for, but I assumed it was for entry to the small zoo park. The animals were lovely. From the driving road, walk to the left—along the road it’s much cleaner. It takes about 30 minutes to reach the red rock formations. There are a few selling points along the way. When you see a small ascent, climb the hill for a good view from the top.

reviews.author: Fuzumbrhan N
reviews.text: The Fairy Stream in Mui Ne is a nice and easy activity if you’re in the area. The walk starts off quite simple and pleasant, with a shallow stream and soft sand under your feet. At first, the scenery is interesting but not spectacular. However, if you keep walking all the way to the end, you’ll be rewarded. The landscape gradually becomes more impressive, and the highlight is a beautiful view of the red, canyon-like cliffs. This part makes the visit worthwhile and gives the place its unique character. Overall, it’s a relaxing experience and worth doing, especially if you take the time to go all the way to the end.

reviews.author: Максим Прилепский
reviews.text: If you ride a bike, you will not assume that there is a beautiful stream here, I have passed by several times. The place is worth a visit, an interesting canyon, in some places you need to walk barefoot along the stream, you can leave your slippers in the clay. But take your slippers with you, there are places where there is garbage at the bottom, put on shoes there. It's very beautiful in sunny weather. There are also small tents at the exit closer to the sea, where they sell fruits and souvenirs. It is easier to follow the stream from top to bottom, start where the bypass road from Phan Thiet to Mui Ne is, there is a cafe with a mini park on the veranda, from this park there is a descent to the waterfall. If you want to see the waterfall at the end, then you need to go up from the sea. U had been walked around 30 minutes

reviews.author: Elliot Byrne
reviews.text: I don't recommend walking up from the beach, not maintained, trashy, a bit sketchy. But from first bridge to waterfall is very nice. Getting there early means no crowds, 7am. You can jog back along a gravel trail if bring shoes, up stairs from waterfall, exit right and uphill to main gravel road, follow it to beach road and then find an alley to the beach. Beach can run on barefoot. Faerie river definitely barefoot. Shaded. Nice walk.

reviews.author: Sunny Sunny
reviews.text: Explore the Fairy Stream while you’re here — it’s a unique experience. Just a heads up: some sections can be tricky to walk barefoot as there are a few sharp rocks along the way. Best to come prepared with slippers for a more comfortable stroll.

reviews.author: Anže Martinčič
reviews.text: Nice experience walking up the stream - It is worth to check at least first 20min of the path. You'll see some incredible red & white sandstone and clay formations that are estimated to be 8000 year old. Be careful, on some parts the water is knee deep but you can avoid them. You can leave the shoes at the entrance but we preffered to take them with us. But it is not worth to go all the way to the waterfall (35min walk) because It turns out the "waterfall" is just a sad, brown cascade of questionable origin that smells suspiciously and flows directly into the water you are walking in. Good thing is that if you reach that point you can get out to the road and call grab so you don't have to walk back again.

reviews.author: Nataliia Bohdanets
reviews.text: Be careful!!!!! My husband and I were also deceived. We were told to buy tickets for 50,000 VND each, but it turned out to be a ticket to a zoo where the animals look very sad. Please don’t support this business. Look for an entrance without a fee or without paying for the zoo. Don’t support businesses that exploit animals. The canyon is very beautiful

reviews.author: Niv Yefet
reviews.text: ** The 4 Places jeep tour** The tour was disappointing from start to finish, in every aspect! At the agency, they described it as a 4 hour Jeep tour. We stopped at four places the fishing village, the fairy stream, the red dunes, and the white dunes. The first two stops, the fishing village and the stream, felt like quick photo stops just because they were on the way to the dunes, which are clearly the main part of the tour. They offered us a shared Jeep option at 1:00 PM or 4:00 PM for around 100,000–150,000 VND per person (I don’t remember the exact price, but around that). If you want to go in the morning, which is actually better for the experience and the weather, you can only do it with a private Jeep for 275,000 VND per person. The whole thing felt like stop after stop just to take pictures, not a real experience. The stop at the fairy stream was okay but nothing special, not something we’d consider a must. The fishing village was also just fine, we stopped, took a picture, and left. The beach there was dirty and not pleasant to walk around. We expected to see a real fishing village and experience the place, but that didn’t happen. Then we went to the white dunes, where we expected to enter with the Jeep we came with since we paid for a Jeep tour. At the agency, they told us that renting a vehicle at the dunes is optional for an extra fee, but they never said it was mandatory or that the Jeep we arrived in wouldn’t go inside. They made it sound like a bonus, not a must. When we arrived, we found out that you can’t enter the white dunes without paying extra either by a big Jeep (800,000 VND) or an ATV for two people (600,000 VND). We paid 600,000 VND for just a 10-minute ride around the dunes and the lake, completely unreasonable. We already paid for a Jeep tour, but in reality, the Jeep was just a taxi from stop to stop. The least they could do is inform people in advance that you’re required to pay extra to enter the dunes. It’s a big amount and should definitely be mentioned beforehand! You can also do sandboarding at the dunes, but of course, that’s another extra charge. Overall, the stops were disappointing. We expected much more, especially for the price we paid. The views and the experience felt very basic, mostly just quick photo breaks, and it was frustrating to have to pay even more during the tour. At the end, it’s also customary to tip the driver (around 150,000–200,000 VND, from what we understood). Our recommendation check for another way to visit these places and save your money. I’ve attached photos so you can see what it really looks like. What we saw in person was nothing like what was described by the agency or shown on Google. In short, it’s not a must do. If I had known in advance, I would have found another way to get there, maybe by motorbike or any other means, or just enjoyed a relaxing beach day instead. In total, we paid 1,350,000 VND for this tour. It was okay, but definitely not worth that price. If the price had been fair, I wouldn’t complain, but for two people, it’s just not worth it. We left at 8:00 AM and got back to the hotel at 11:10 AM. Most of the tour was just driving in the Jeep with short photo stops. Overall, we don’t recommend it. This was our honest personal experience.

reviews.author: RONA
reviews.text: A magical stream for an easy, refreshing walk on a hot day. The water reaches just below the knees, and the soft sand is perfect for walking barefoot. There’s a spot to leave your shoes at the start of the trail. Entrance is free. It’s a wonderful place for kids, with colorful sands, trees, and plants along the way. You can also find places to buy drinks, and even vendors who try to make you pose with a snake – which is part of the experience.

reviews.author: Natalija Kert
reviews.text: It’s an okay experience, but nothing special. You basically walk through muddy water, and the beginning is quite disappointing because there is a lot of trash around. However, it slowly gets prettier as you walk further in. If you are in the area, go give it a try for the experience, but don't expect anything extraordinary.

reviews.author: Nilou Kedemos
reviews.text: Really pretty once you get past the initial bad smell and rubbish at the start. The main part is well taken care of and there are some awesome photo opportunities. Wear sand shoes if you can as there are many sharp rocks that you can’t see in the stream since the water is brown/red at points. We also saw a couple snakes which was a bit scary but we didn’t get bitten!

reviews.author: Ashley T (Shark and Eagle)
reviews.text: The fairy stream is a very unique place to walk around. But I want to say be careful if you drive motorbike here by yourself, the Vietnamese guy live at picture 1 and picture 2 will demanding your park at his place ( it’s illegal to force some one to do so) and if he see your parked somewhere else e.g. 50 meters away on the side of the road, you will be aggressively yelled at. It happened to me and ruined my day. He yelled and swore and threaten me and asked me to leave, tbh it looks like he is gonna kill me. The fact is, this place belongs its nation, not privately owned by someone. They have no rights to tell you what to and not to do. I have full respect for Vietnam and Vietnamese people but I won’t tolerate abusive behavior. I warn people who come visit this place do not give in for force. E.g. there are few entrance, like the one opposite of the road if you want to avoid this guy.

reviews.author: AY
reviews.text: The "Grand" Canyon of Mud We stripped off our shoes for a "magical" hike, But waded through silt in a glorified dyke. Halfway through the sludge, we just lost the spark; It’s hard to find wonder in lukewarm red bark. If a three-inch "waterfall" calls to your soul, Then soldier on through to that miniature goal. But a man with a constrictor? A massive "Big Nope." I’ve run out of interest and run out of hope. It’s cruddy, it’s hyped, and it’s vastly overrated, A "scenic" disaster that left us frustrated. Keep your red sand and the grime on your feet, I’m done with the stream; it is screw it, complete.

reviews.author: Bok “Doc Bok” McDonagh
reviews.text: Resting at the bottom of the Red Canyon is the Fairy Stream. Famous for its red sand and barefoot river walk, it is often referred to as Vietnam's Grand Canyon in miniature. The entrance to the Fairy Stream is to the north of the main road leading into Mui Ne. At the entrance there is a small restaurant where you can enjoy Vietnamese coffee and local food while watching the river below. It cost 5,000 dong to enter in 2024, and you can leave your shoes at the entrance. I recommend walking the river barefoot. The sand is soft, and the water is warm, so it is comfortable to walk down. Bring a towel so you can dry yourself off afterwards! Trees, grass and other plant life line the stream, glowing a bright green under the sun and contrasting orange-red river. There were a few places to take a rest, or for photo opportunities, including a swing beneath the canyon's walls. The plant life eventually gives way to impressive limestone formations embedded in the red clay cliffs. These are impressive to see up close and above ground. There is a large cluster of stalagmites that look like small statues that have been weathered over the years. Past the limestone formations, the flora returns, lining the canyon with leaves. A small restaurant is set up here, but it was closed and empty on the day I visited. Despite the cliffs being left behind, there are still signs of limestone, with small formations dotted around. One that stood out to me had a strange colour and looked more like a termite's nest. At the end of the canyon, there is a small waterfall. This is the source of the Fairy Stream Don't get too close, I was warned by a local that you can get swept under if you do. If you're spending time in Mui Ne, this is a must do. The short walk takes less than half an hour, but you can spend more time here if you want to take in the scenery.

reviews.author: Chan Nguyen (Yaki Chan)
reviews.text: It definitely had more water flowing today Nov 19, 2025. It was more than 1 foot deep around some rooks. As I walked in the stream from south to north, it got kind of tough in some places. Some people gave up and turned around but I continued on further toward the waterfall but I also gave up and got out at a "restaurant". Climbed up and out. Took a Moto taxi back. Never saw the waterfalls. View after my exit point.

reviews.author: Yau Xue Yen
reviews.text: You have to take off your shoes and walk on the stream, I wouldn't suggest to go if you have kid / elderly because there's really tendency to fall down/scratched by stone. But it was a special experience 😆 and there's white snake to take pic too haha

reviews.author: Chris Prettejohn
reviews.text: It's exactly what it says it is, a walk along a stream. Once you've dodged the snake handlers and get past the trash laden start it's actually quite nice although interspersed with the occasional karaoke bar and occasional litter dump. It'll take about 30 minutes before you get to the waterfall which is quite cute but don't look too long into the plunge pool as there appears to be a lot of effluent swirling around. This country is great but going through the growing pains of mass tourism and this represents it. You can also take the side walk up the red dune for a great view across Mui Ne from the top. Definetly worth it as a fun afternoon excursion.

reviews.author: F. Giovannie S.
reviews.text: We were bored and it was just few minutes of walking from our small hotel so we decided to go there. The entrance was free, and the first few meters were very… unconvincing. Lots of trash, smelly, and unappealing. Then you met the snake guy who offered to take pic with yellow python for 50k. Then the more you walk, it got bit better. Basically, it’s really what it is, a walk along the stream. The scene was so-so, not breathtaking but also not ugly. Good water and being in nature fore few minutes is always nice. Not exactly a great experience but if you’re bored, it’s good to try.

reviews.author: L C
reviews.text: Stunning walk and very refreshing ! It was part of my tour with Mui Ne: Best Private Sand Dunes Jeep Tour at Sunrise/Sunset. I booked via Get Your Guide app. Very easy to book up with them.

reviews.author: Juste Feyza
reviews.text: Lack of guidance, missing information, yet the place is a natural beauty. You walk through a stream, clay soil under your feet. There's a tint waterfall waits for you in the end if you bother to walk. I liked it.

reviews.author: Karl Philipp Stoerk
reviews.text: Nice walk through the creek with colorful sand dunes. I preferred to walk barefoot to feel the different types of sand and clay. Easy access from the street right at the bridge, the sign makes it hard to miss. Free entry, just bring a bag for your shoes and little money for a drink along the way.

reviews.author: Lucas (Phan Duc Lap)
reviews.text: Walk along the stream, it's kinda interesting at first but there are some trash that need to be cleaned, not that much but still. Recommend if you want to go for the first time.

reviews.author: Florian Haider
reviews.text: Can't understand the bad reviews - It's free and nice to see. I would recommend bathing shoes

reviews.author: Donnamarie Vanderhorst
reviews.text: A fun walk up the fairy stream. There are a few slippery spots so be careful. But for the most part, it's lovely soft sand and lovely fresh running water. We went barefoot and had no problems. There are unique sand dune formations along the trail and a number of spots for a picture. They even have small bars and cafes to stop for a drink or refreshments. We saw a group enjoying karaoke at one such bar. The other trail walkers were not really enjoying it though haha. The waterfall at the end is picturesque and worth the trek. About 1 hour round trip, at a leisurely pace

reviews.author: T P
reviews.text: It’s free entrance. Yet you have to pay for the parking fee (40k/car). You gotta walk more than a kilometer and i think the most scenic part is the canyon-alike. Elae, you walk under trees. The stream is not clear but cool. Beware of the soft sand

reviews.author: Saskia Kahlan
reviews.text: A unique and beautiful natural attraction with red and white rock formations, combined with the soft, cooling stream, makes for a special experience. The area was clean during my visit, and there were loads of colourful butterflies and dragonflies about. I went on a Saturday at noon—busy on the way to the waterfall but much quieter on the return. Be aware that some people at various entrances try to charge a fee (amounts vary, so they don’t seem official), but it's possible to find free access points. Well worth a visit!

reviews.author: Ks M
reviews.text: Don't buy any permission or ticket....there are no official entrance fees. It's worth a visit, especially early in the morning when the noisy crowds aren't yet around. You can walk to a small waterfall in about 30 minutes, enjoy a coconut for 20,000 dong and walk back. Look at the pictures, it really is a play of colors between red and white and nature.

reviews.author: Tom Harry
reviews.text: Kinda dirty no fairies but the animals seemed happy and was nice to walk down the stream. Watch out for quicksand!

reviews.author: Amira Yusof
reviews.text: Sorry to say if but if you plan to have a day trip at Muinebecause you just want to stop by, I suggest you to skip this place and go to play the ATV and sand dunes first. Because you will spend at least 2 hour hereand it is not worth the journey. I booked private car from HCM- Muine-Dalat. and I already told Klook that at Muine I want to go sand dunes and play ATV. onthe day, the driver said that the first destination is Siu Tien and I just agree because I think if add just one place it should be fine. Also I though we will spend maximum of 1 hour here,but when we start to walk, we are questioning this lead to what and they said towards the end there is a waterfall. The journey to waterfall took 1 hour by walkingwith normal distance with stops to take pictures. Unfortunately the waterfall is not worth not impressive enough after we spend 1 hour walking to thewaterfall. behind the waterfall there is a banana farm and a café if want to rest for a while. Also in front of the entrance also has a restaurant. we only buythe coconut water because we are thirsty.

reviews.author: L R
reviews.text: It's really not worth it. Yes, the walking in the stream is nice, but that's about it. When you get to the end, it's a really disappointing waterfall. Before you get to the end, the water becomes very disgusting. It's worth coming if you just walk for the first section. i wouldn't waste your time walking to the end.
